response.justification: Candidate has relevant IDE development experience from National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI in C/C++) and strong polyglot systems background, but the core requirements — deep JVM expertise, Kotlin, and Spring ecosystem — are largely absent. Java appears as a listed skill with limited, dated hands-on usage (robotic arms and device orchestration ~2016-2018), and there is no Kotlin or Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate experience. Recent work has been TypeScript/Node.js focused, so a JVM tooling role would be a significant pivot. Worth a screening conversation only if pipeline is thin.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience at National Instruments writing C/C++ components for LabWindows/CVI
Track record on complex, large-scale systems (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic millions of req/sec, low-latency async work)
Strong software craftsmanship signals: TDD, CI/CD, workshops, talks, OSS test runner and assertion libraries
concerns: No Kotlin experience and no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate exposure on the resume
Java listed but evidence is thin and dated; primary recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js, not JVM
No demonstrated work on language tooling, compilers, parsers, or modern IDE plugin internals (IntelliJ Platform, LSP, PSI, etc.)
